title: Flowise unauthenticated OAuth2 token refresh endpoint discloses access tokens
description: The route POST /api/v1/oauth2-credential/refresh/:credentialId in packages/server/src/routes/oauth2/index.ts performs no authentication or authorization check: it loads the credential solely by the credentialId path parameter, refreshes the OAuth2 token, and returns it via tokenInfo: { ...tokenData, ... }. The route prefix '/api/v1/oauth2-credential/refresh' is in WHITELIST_URLS, so the global auth middleware does not protect it. Because credential identifiers leak from the also-unauthenticated GET /api/v1/public-chatbotConfig/:chatflowId (its flowData includes the 'credential' field), an unauthenticated attacker can chain the two endpoints to obtain valid OAuth2 access tokens for third-party services configured in a public chatflow.
